methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Osmo is a Series B consumer tech company founded by Alex Wiltschko that has raised $130M to date. The valuation exercise relies on a limited methodological toolkit due to the absence of comparable public or private peers in the data set; no comp universe is available for cross-reference.

The stage-progression method is the sole applicable approach here. Using total capital raised ($130M) as a baseline value proxy and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $247M. This method assumes Osmo's growth trajectory and risk profile align with historical Series B progression patterns in consumer tech. No alternative triangulation methods—such as revenue multiples, DCF, or peer benchmarking—could be applied given data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $210M (low) to $284M (high), with a median point of $247M. This range reflects sensitivity around the stage-progression multiplier rather than convergence across independent methods. The single-method foundation and complete absence of comp data represent material constraints on confidence. Key uncertainties include Osmo's revenue run rate, unit economics, and growth rate relative to peers, none of which are provided. A more robust valuation would require either comparable transaction data in consumer tech or revenue-based metrics to validate or adjust the stage-progression output.
